HTTP transport with MapMcp and implement a resource.

arguments.name: explore-mcp-api
arguments.prompt:
In the NuGet package cache, find the ModelContextProtocol and ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore assemblies (version 0.2.0-preview*) and list their public API surface. I need to know:

1. How to register MCP server in DI (AddMcpServer and its builder methods like WithTools, WithResources, etc.)
2. How to map the HTTP endpoint (MapMcp)
3. How to define tools using attributes ([McpServerTool], [McpServerToolType], etc.)
4. How to define resources using attributes ([McpServerResource], [McpServerResourceType], [McpServerResourceTemplate], etc.)
5. What resource-related interfaces/classes exist (McpServerResource, ResourceTemplate, etc.)

Search in ~/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/ and ~/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.aspnetcore/ for source or decompiled XML docs. Also look for any README or docs files in the package.

Focus on finding the exact API signatures and attribute names.

result.content:
# ASP.NET Core extensions for the MCP C# SDK

[![NuGet preview version](https://img.shields.io/nuget/vpre/ModelContextProtocol.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol/absoluteLatest)

The official C# SDK for the [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/), enabling .NET applications, services, and libraries to implement and interact with MCP clients and servers. Please visit our [API documentation](https://modelcontextprotocol.github.io/csharp-sdk/api/ModelContextProtocol.html) for more details on available functionality.

> [!NOTE]
> This project is in preview; breaking changes can be introduced without prior notice.

## About MCP

The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open protocol that standardizes how applications provide context to Large Language Models (LLMs). It enables secure integration between LLMs and various data sources and tools.

For more information about MCP:

- [Official Documentation](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/)
- [Protocol Specification](https://spec.modelcontextprotocol.io/)
- [GitHub Organization](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol)

## Installation

To get started, install the package from NuGet

```
dotnet new web
dotnet add package ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore --prerelease
```

## Getting Started

```csharp
// Program.cs
using ModelContextProtocol.Server;
using System.ComponentModel;

var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);
builder.Services.AddMcpServer()
    .WithHttpTransport()
    .WithToolsFromAssembly();
var app = builder.Build();

app.MapMcp();

app.Run("http://localhost:3001");

[McpServerToolType]
public static class EchoTool
{
    [McpServerTool, Description("Echoes the message back to the client.")]
    public static string Echo(string message) => $"hello {message}";
}
```

            <summary>
            Default implementation of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder"/> that enables fluent configuration
            of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. This builder is returned by the
            <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerServiceCollectionExtensions.AddMcpServer(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,System.Action{ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions})"/> extension method and
            provides access to the service collection for registering additional MCP components.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.DefaultMcpServerBuilder.Services">
            <inheritdoc/>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.DefaultMcpServerBuilder.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection)">
            <summary>
            Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.DefaultMcpServerBuilder"/> class.
            </summary>
            <param name="services">The service collection to which MCP server services will be added. This collection
            is exposed through the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.DefaultMcpServerBuilder.Services"/> property to allow additional configuration.</param>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException">Thrown when <paramref name="services"/> is null.</exception>
        </member>

            <summary>

            <remarks>
            <para>
            The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder"/> interface provides a fluent API for configuring Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers
            when using dependency injection. It exposes methods for registering tools, prompts, custom request handlers,
            and server transports, allowing for comprehensive server configuration through a chain of method calls.
            </para>

<member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.DefaultMcpServerBuilder">

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<doc>
    <assembly>
        <name>ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore</name>
    </assembly>
    <members>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions">
            <summary>
            Provides methods for configuring HTTP MCP servers via dependency injection.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions.WithHttpTransport(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Action{ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions})">
            <summary>
            Adds the services necessary for <see cref="M:McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions.MapMcp"/>
            to handle MCP requests and sessions using the MCP Streamable HTTP transport. For more information on configuring the underlying HTTP server
            to control things like port binding custom TLS certificates, see the <see href="https://learn.microsoft.com/aspnet/core/fundamentals/minimal-apis">Minimal APIs quick reference</see>.
            </summary>
            <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
            <param name="configureOptions">Configures options for the Streamable HTTP transport. This allows configuring per-session
            <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions"/> and running logic before and after a session.</param>
            <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions">
            <summary>
            Provides extension methods for <see cref="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing.IEndpointRouteBuilder"/> to add MCP endpoints.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions.MapMcp(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing.IEndpointRouteBuilder,System.String)">
            <summary>
            Sets up endpoints for handling MCP Streamable HTTP transport.
            See <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-03-26/basic/transports#streamable-http">the 2025-03-26 protocol specification</see> for details about the Streamable HTTP transport.
            Also maps legacy SSE endpoints for backward compatibility at the path "/sse" and "/message". <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2024-11-05/basic/transports#http-with-sse">the 2024-11-05 protocol specification</see> for details about the HTTP with SSE transport.
            </summary>
            <param name="endpoints">The web application to attach MCP HTTP endpoints.</param>
            <param name="pattern">The route pattern prefix to map to.</param>
            <returns>Returns a builder for configuring additional endpoint conventions like authorization policies.</returns>
        </member>
        <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions">
            <summary>
            Configuration options for <see cref="M:McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions.MapMcp"/>.
            which implements the Streaming HTTP transport for the Model Context Protocol.
            See the protocol specification for details on the Streamable HTTP transport. <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-03-26/basic/transports#streamable-http"/>
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.ConfigureSessionOptions">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets an optional asynchronous callback to configure per-session <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions"/>
            with access to the <see cref="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.HttpContext"/> of the request that initiated the session.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.RunSessionHandler">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets an optional asynchronous callback for running new MCP sessions manually.
            This is useful for running logic before a sessions starts and after it completes.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.Stateless">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets whether the server should run in a stateless mode that does not require all requests for a given session
            to arrive to the same ASP.NET Core application process.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            If <see langword="true"/>, the "/sse" endpoint will be disabled, and client information will be round-tripped as part
            of the "mcp-session-id" header instead of stored in memory. Unsolicited server-to-client messages and all server-to-client
            requests are also unsupported, because any responses may arrive at another ASP.NET Core application process.
            Client sampling and roots capabilities are also disabled in stateless mode, because the server cannot make requests.
            Defaults to <see langword="false"/>.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.IdleTimeout">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the duration of time the server will wait between any active requests before timing out an MCP session.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            This is checked in background every 5 seconds. A client trying to resume a session will receive a 404 status code
            and should restart their session. A client can keep their session open by keeping a GET request open.
            Defaults to 2 hours.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.MaxIdleSessionCount">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets maximum number of idle sessions to track in memory. This is used to limit the number of sessions that can be idle at once.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            Past this limit, the server will log a critical error and terminate the oldest idle sessions even if they have not reached
            their <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.IdleTimeout"/> until the idle session count is below this limit. Clients that keep their session open by
            keeping a GET request open will not count towards this limit.
            Defaults to 100,000 sessions.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.TimeProvider">
            <summary>
            Used for testing the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.IdleTimeout"/>.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.Stateless.StatelessSessionIdJsonContext.StatelessSessionId">
            <summary>
            Defines the source generated JSON serialization contract metadata for a given type.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.Stateless.StatelessSessionIdJsonContext.UserIdClaim">
            <summary>
            Defines the source generated JSON serialization contract metadata for a given type.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.Stateless.StatelessSessionIdJsonContext.Implementation">
            <summary>
            Defines the source generated JSON serialization contract metadata for a given type.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.Stateless.StatelessSessionIdJsonContext.String">
            <summary>
            Defines the source generated JSON serialization contract metadata for a given type.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.Stateless.StatelessSessionIdJsonContext.Default">
            <summary>
            The default <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.Serialization.JsonSerializerContext"/> associated with a default <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions"/> instance.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.Stateless.StatelessSessionIdJsonContext.GeneratedSerializerOptions">
            <summary>
            The source-generated options associated with this context.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.Stateless.StatelessSessionIdJsonContext.#ctor">
            <inheritdoc/>
        </member>
        <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.Stateless.StatelessSessionIdJsonContext.#ctor(System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)">
            <inheritdoc/>
        </member>
        <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.Stateless.StatelessSessionIdJsonContext.GetTypeInfo(System.Type)">
            <inheritdoc/>
        </member>
    </members>
</doc>

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<doc>
    <assembly>
        <name>ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore</name>
    </assembly>
    <members>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions">
            <summary>
            Provides methods for configuring HTTP MCP servers via dependency injection.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions.WithHttpTransport(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Action{ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions})">
            <summary>
            Adds the services necessary for <see cref="M:McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions.MapMcp"/>
            to handle MCP requests and sessions using the MCP Streamable HTTP transport. For more information on configuring the underlying HTTP server
            to control things like port binding custom TLS certificates, see the <see href="https://learn.microsoft.com/aspnet/core/fundamentals/minimal-apis">Minimal APIs quick reference</see>.
            </summary>
            <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
            <param name="configureOptions">Configures options for the Streamable HTTP transport. This allows configuring per-session
            <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions"/> and running logic before and after a session.</param>
            <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions">
            <summary>
            Provides extension methods for <see cref="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing.IEndpointRouteBuilder"/> to add MCP endpoints.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions.MapMcp(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing.IEndpointRouteBuilder,System.String)">
            <summary>
            Sets up endpoints for handling MCP Streamable HTTP transport.
            See <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-03-26/basic/transports#streamable-http">the 2025-03-26 protocol specification</see> for details about the Streamable HTTP transport.
            Also maps legacy SSE endpoints for backward compatibility at the path "/sse" and "/message". <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2024-11-05/basic/transports#http-with-sse">the 2024-11-05 protocol specification</see> for details about the HTTP with SSE transport.
            </summary>
            <param name="endpoints">The web application to attach MCP HTTP endpoints.</param>
            <param name="pattern">The route pattern prefix to map to.</param>
            <returns>Returns a builder for configuring additional endpoint conventions like authorization policies.</returns>
        </member>
        <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions">
            <summary>
            Configuration options for <see cref="M:McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions.MapMcp"/>.
            which implements the Streaming HTTP transport for the Model Context Protocol.
            See the protocol specification for details on the Streamable HTTP transport. <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-03-26/basic/transports#streamable-http"/>
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.ConfigureSessionOptions">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets an optional asynchronous callback to configure per-session <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions"/>
            with access to the <see cref="T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.HttpContext"/> of the request that initiated the session.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.RunSessionHandler">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets an optional asynchronous callback for running new MCP sessions manually.
            This is useful for running logic before a sessions starts and after it completes.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.Stateless">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets whether the server should run in a stateless mode that does not require all requests for a given session
            to arrive to the same ASP.NET Core application process.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            If <see langword="true"/>, the "/sse" endpoint will be disabled, and client information will be round-tripped as part
            of the "mcp-session-id" header instead of stored in memory. Unsolicited server-to-client messages and all server-to-client
            requests are also unsupported, because any responses may arrive at another ASP.NET Core application process.
            Client sampling and roots capabilities are also disabled in stateless mode, because the server cannot make requests.
            Defaults to <see langword="false"/>.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.IdleTimeout">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the duration of time the server will wait between any active requests before timing out an MCP session.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            This is checked in background every 5 seconds. A client trying to resume a session will receive a 404 status code
            and should restart their session. A client can keep their session open by keeping a GET request open.
            Defaults to 2 hours.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.MaxIdleSessionCount">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets maximum number of idle sessions to track in memory. This is used to limit the number of sessions that can be idle at once.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            Past this limit, the server will log a critical error and terminate the oldest idle sessions even if they have not reached
            their <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.IdleTimeout"/> until the idle session count is below this limit. Clients that keep their session open by
            keeping a GET request open will not count towards this limit.
            Defaults to 100,000 sessions.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.TimeProvider">
            <summary>
            Used for testing the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.IdleTimeout"/>.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.Stateless.StatelessSessionIdJsonContext.StatelessSessionId">
            <summary>
            Defines the source generated JSON serialization contract metadata for a given type.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.Stateless.StatelessSessionIdJsonContext.UserIdClaim">

            </summary>
        </member>

            </summary>
        </member>

            </summary>
        </member>

data.content: Let me use Python to extract and format the XML documentation more efficiently:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
import re

# Parse the XML doc file
xml_file = "/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/0.2.0-preview.3/lib/net9.0/ModelContextProtocol.xml"
tree = ET.parse(xml_file)
root = tree.getroot()

# Extract all members
members = {}
for member in root.findall('.//member'):
    name = member.get('name', '')
    summary_elem = member.find('summary')
    summary = summary_elem.text.strip() if summary_elem is not None and summary_elem.text else ""
    
    # Get remarks
    remarks_elem = member.find('remarks')
    remarks = remarks_elem.text.strip() if remarks_elem is not None and remarks_elem.text else ""
    
    members[name] = {'summary': summary, 'remarks': remarks}

# Focus on key items
patterns = [
    r'T:ModelContextProtocol\.Server\.McpServer',
    r'T:Microsoft\.Extensions\.DependencyInjection.*McpServer',
    r'M:Microsoft\.Extensions\.DependencyInjection.*\.(With|Add)',
]

for name, data in sorted(members.items()):
    # Filter for relevant items
    if any(re.search(p, name) for p in patterns):
        print(f"\n{name}")
        if data['summary']:
            print(f"  Summary: {data['summary'][:150]}...")
EOF

            in the <paramref name="builder"/>'s <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection"/>.
            </para>
            <para>
            The method automatically handles both static and instance members. For instance members, a new instance
            of the containing class will be constructed for each invocation of the resource.
            </para>
            <para>
            Resource templates registered through this method can be discovered by clients using the <c>list_resourceTemplates</c> request
            and invoked using the <c>read_resource</c> request.
            </para>
            <para>
            Note that this method performs reflection at runtime and may not work in Native AOT scenarios. For
            Native AOT compatibility, consider using the generic <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithResources``1(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder)"/> method instead.
            </para>

cat ~/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/0.2.0-preview.3/lib/net9.0/ModelContextProtocol.xml | python3 << 'PYEOF'
import sys
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

content = sys.stdin.read()
root = ET.fromstring(content)

# Find all members and group them
types_dict = {}
methods_dict = {}

for member in root.findall('.//member'):
    name = member.get('name', '')
    summary_elem = member.find('summary')
    summary = (summary_elem.text or '').strip() if summary_elem is not None else ''
    
    if name.startswith('T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.'):
        typename = name[2:]
        types_dict[typename] = summary
    elif 'McpServerBuilderExtensions' in name and name.startswith('M:'):
        method_name = name[2:]
        methods_dict[method_name] = summary

print("=" * 80)
print("KEY TYPES IN ModelContextProtocol.Server")
print("=" * 80)
for t in sorted(types_dict.keys()):
    cls_name = t.split('.')[-1]
    print(f"\n{cls_name}")
    if types_dict[t]:
        print(f"  {types_dict[t][:100]}...")

print("\n" + "=" * 80)
print("BUILDER EXTENSION METHODS")
print("=" * 80)
for m in sorted(methods_dict.keys()):
    if '.With' in m or '.Add' in m:
        parts = m.split('.')
        method_part = parts[-1]
        # Extract just method name without generics/params
        method_name = method_part.split('(')[0].split('`')[0]
        print(f"\n{method_name}")
        if methods_dict[m]:
            print(f"  {methods_dict[m][:100]}...")

385.         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithSubscribeToResourcesHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Func{ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SubscribeRequestParams},System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmptyResult}})">
386.             <summary>
387.             Configures a handler for resource subscription requests.
388.             </summary>
389.             <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
390.             <param name="handler">The handler function that processes resource subscription requests.</param>
391.             <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
392.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
393.             <remarks>
394.             <para>
395.             The subscribe handler is responsible for registering client interest in specific resources. When a resource
396.             changes, the server can notify all subscribed clients about the change.
397.             </para>
398.             <para>
399.             This handler is typically paired with <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithUnsubscribeFromResourcesHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Func{ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.UnsubscribeRequestParams},System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmptyResult}})"/> to provide a complete
400.             subscription management system. Resource subscriptions allow clients to maintain up-to-date information without
401.             needing to poll resources constantly.
402.             </para>
403.             <para>
404.             After registering a subscription, it's the server's responsibility to track which client is subscribed to which
405.             resources and to send appropriate notifications through the connection when resources change.
406.             </para>
407.             </remarks>
408.         </member>

410.             <summary>

412.             </summary>
413.             <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
414.             <param name="handler">The handler function that processes resource unsubscription requests.</param>
415.             <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
416.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
417.             <remarks>
418.             <para>
419.             The unsubscribe handler is responsible for removing client interest in specific resources. When a client
420.             no longer needs to receive notifications about resource changes, it can send an unsubscribe request.
421.             </para>
422.             <para>
423.             This handler is typically paired with <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithSubscribeToResourcesHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Func{ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SubscribeRequestParams},System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmptyResult}})"/> to provide a complete
424.             subscription management system. The unsubscribe operation is idempotent, meaning it can be called multiple
425.             times for the same resource without causing errors, even if there is no active subscription.

502.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers">
503.             <summary>
504.             Provides a container for handlers used in the creation of an MCP server.
505.             </summary>
506.             <remarks>
507.             <para>
508.             This class provides a centralized collection of delegates that implement various capabilities of the Model Context Protocol.
509.             Each handler in this class corresponds to a specific endpoint in the Model Context Protocol and
510.             is responsible for processing a particular type of request. The handlers are used to customize
511.             the behavior of the MCP server by providing implementations for the various protocol operations.
512.             </para>
513.             <para>
514.             Handlers can be configured individually using the extension methods in <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions"/>
515.             such as <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithListToolsHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Func{ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListToolsRequestParams},System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListToolsResult}})"/> and
516.             <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithCallToolHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Func{ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams},System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResponse}})"/>.
517.             </para>
518.             <para>
519.             When a client sends a request to the server, the appropriate handler is invoked to process the
520.             request and produce a response according to the protocol specification. Which handler is selected
521.             is done based on an ordinal, case-sensitive string comparison.
522.             </para>
523.             </remarks>
524.         </member>
525.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.ListToolsHandler">
526.             <summary>
527.             Gets or sets the handler for <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ToolsList"/> requests.
528.             </summary>
529.             <remarks>
530.             <para>
531.             The handler should return a list of available tools when requested by a client.
532.             It supports pagination through the cursor mechanism, where the client can make
533.             repeated calls with the cursor returned by the previous call to retrieve more tools.
534.             </para>
535.             <para>
536.             This handler works alongside any tools defined in the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> collection.
537.             Tools from both sources will be combined when returning results to clients.
538.             </para>
539.             </remarks>
540.         </member>

             Handlers can be configured individually using the extension methods in <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions"/>
             such as <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithListToolsHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Func{ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListToolsRequestParams},System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListToolsResult}})"/> and
             <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithCallToolHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Func{ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams},System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResponse}})"/>.
             </para>
             <para>
             When a client sends a request to the server, the appropriate handler is invoked to process the
             request and produce a response according to the protocol specification. Which handler is selected
             is done based on an ordinal, case-sensitive string comparison.
             </para>
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.ListToolsHandler">
             <summary>
             Gets or sets the handler for <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ToolsList"/> requests.
             </summary>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             The handler should return a list of available tools when requested by a client.
             It supports pagination through the cursor mechanism, where the client can make
             repeated calls with the cursor returned by the previous call to retrieve more tools.
             </para>
             <para>
             This handler works alongside any tools defined in the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> collection.
             Tools from both sources will be combined when returning results to clients.
             </para>
             </remarks>

             <remarks>

             The handler should implement logic to execute the requested tool and return appropriate results.
             </remarks>
         </member>

             <summary>

             </summary>

             </para>

             </para>
             </remarks>
         </member>

             </summary>

             The handler should implement logic to fetch or generate the requested prompt and return appropriate results.
             </remarks>
         </member>

33.             Also maps legacy SSE endpoints for backward compatibility at the path "/sse" and "/message". <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2024-11-05/basic/transports#http-with-sse">the 2024-11-05 protocol specification</see> for details about the HTTP with SSE transport.

61.             to arrive to the same ASP.NET Core application process.
62.             </summary>

65.             of the "mcp-session-id" header instead of stored in memory. Unsolicited server-to-client messages and all server-to-client
66.             requests are also unsupported, because any responses may arrive at another ASP.NET Core application process.

68.             Defaults to <see langword="false"/>.

<member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResourceAttribute">
            <summary>
            Used to indicate that a method or property should be considered an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource"/>.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            This attribute is applied to methods or properties that should be exposed as resources in the Model Context Protocol. When a class 
            containing methods marked with this attribute is registered with McpServerBuilderExtensions,
            these methods or properties become available as resources that can be called by MCP clients.
            </para>
            <para>
            When methods are provided directly to <see cref="M:McpServerResource.Create"/>, the attribute is not required.
            </para>
            <para>
            Read resource requests do not contain separate arguments, only a URI. However, for templated resources, portions of that URI may be considered
            as arguments and may be bound to parameters. Further, resource methods may accept parameters that will be bound to arguments based on their type.
            <list type="bullet">
              <item>
                <description>
                  <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameters are automatically bound to a <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> provided by the
                  <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.IMcpServer"/> and that respects any <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelledNotification"/>s sent by the client for this operation's
<exited with exit code 0>

import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

xml_file = "/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.core/0.2.0-preview.3/lib/net9.0/ModelContextProtocol.Core.xml"
tree = ET.parse(xml_file)
root = tree.getroot()

# Find specific types we care about
target_types = [
    "McpServerToolAttribute",
    "McpServerToolTypeAttribute",
    "McpServerResourceAttribute",
    "McpServerResourceTypeAttribute",
    "McpServerPromptAttribute",
    "McpServerPromptTypeAttribute",
    "McpServerTool",
    "McpServerResource",
    "McpServerPrompt",
]

for member in root.findall('.//member'):
    name = member.get('name', '')
    
    for target in target_types:
        if f'T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.{target}' == name:
            print(f"\n{'=' * 80}")
            print(f"{target}")
            print('=' * 80)
            
            # Get summary
            summary = member.findtext('summary', '').strip()
            if summary:
                print(f"Summary: {summary[:300]}")
            
            # Get remarks
            remarks = member.findtext('remarks', '').strip()
            if remarks:
                print(f"\nRemarks: {remarks[:300]}")
            
            # Get params
            params = member.findall('param')
            if params:
                print("\nParameters:")
                for p in params:
                    pname = p.get('name', '')
                    ptext = p.text.strip() if p.text else ''
                    print(f"  {pname}: {ptext[:100]}")

PYEOF

7853.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute">
7854.             <summary>
7855.             Used to indicate that a method should be considered an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>.
7856.             </summary>
7857.             <remarks>
7858.             <para>
7859.             This attribute is applied to methods that should be exposed as tools in the Model Context Protocol. When a class 
7860.             containing methods marked with this attribute is registered with McpServerBuilderExtensions,
7861.             these methods become available as tools that can be called by MCP clients.
7862.             </para>
7863.             <para>
7864.             When methods are provided directly to <see cref="M:McpServerTool.Create"/>, the attribute is not required.
7865.             </para>
7866.             <para>
7867.             By default, parameters are sourced from the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams.Arguments"/> dictionary, which is a collection
7868.             of key/value pairs, and are represented in the JSON schema for the function, as exposed in the returned <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>'s
7869.             <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool.ProtocolTool"/>'s <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool.InputSchema"/>. Those parameters are deserialized from the
7870.             <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/> values in that collection. There are a few exceptions to this:
7871.             <list type="bullet">
7872.               <item>
7873.                 <description>
7874.                   <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameters are automatically bound to a <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> provided by the
7875.                   <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.IMcpServer"/> and that respects any <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelledNotification"/>s sent by the client for this operation's
7876.                   <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestId"/>. The parameter is not included in the generated JSON schema.
7877.                 </description>
7878.               </item>
7879.               <item>
7880.                 <description>
7881.                   <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> parameters are bound from the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext`1"/> for this request,
7882.                   and are not included in the JSON schema.
7883.                 </description>
7884.               </item>
7885.               <item>
7886.                 <description>
7887.                   <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.IMcpServer"/> parameters are not included in the JSON schema and are bound directly to the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.IMcpServer"/>
7888.                   instance associated with this request's <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext`1"/>. Such parameters may be used to understand
7889.                   what server is being used to process the request, and to interact with the client issuing the request to that server.
7890.                 </description>
7891.               </item>
7892.               <item>
7893.                 <description>
7894.                   <see cref="T:System.IProgress`1"/> parameters accepting <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.ProgressNotificationValue"/> values
7895.                   are not included in the JSON schema and are bound to an <see cref="T:System.IProgress`1"/> instance manufactured
7896.                   to forward progress notifications from the tool to the client. If the client included a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressToken"/> in their request, 
7897.                   progress reports issued to this instance will propagate to the client as <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ProgressNotification"/> notifications with
7898.                   that token. If the client did not include a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressToken"/>, the instance will ignore any progress reports issued to it.
7899.                 </description>
7900.               </item>
7901.               <item>
7902.                 <description>
7903.                   When the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> is constructed, it may be passed an <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> via 
7904.                   <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolCreateOptions.Services"/>. Any parameter that can be satisfied by that <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/>
7905.                   according to <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceProviderIsService"/> will not be included in the generated JSON schema and will be resolved 
7906.                   from the <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> provided to when the tool is invoked rather than from the argument collection.
7907.                 </description>
7908.               </item>
7909.               <item>
7910.                 <description>
7911.                   Any parameter attributed with <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.FromKeyedServicesAttribute"/> will similarly be resolved from the 
7912.                   <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> provided when the tool is invoked rather than from the argument
7913.                   collection, and will not be included in the generated JSON schema.
7914.                 </description>
7915.               </item>
7916.             </list>
7917.             </para>
7918.             <para>
7919.             All other parameters are deserialized from the <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/>s in the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams.Arguments"/> dictionary, 
7920.             using the <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions"/> supplied in <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolCreateOptions.SerializerOptions"/>, or if none was provided, 
7921.             using <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.McpJsonUtilities.DefaultOptions"/>.
7922.             </para>
7923.             <para>
7924.             In general, the data supplied via the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams.Arguments"/>'s dictionary is passed along from the caller and
7925.             should thus be considered unvalidated and untrusted. To provide validated and trusted data to the invocation of the tool, consider having 
7926.             the tool be an instance method, referring to data stored in the instance, or using an instance or parameters resolved from the <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/>
7927.             to provide data to the method.
7928.             </para>
7929.             <para>
7930.             Return values from a method are used to create the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResponse"/> that is sent back to the client:
7931.             </para>
7932.             <list type="table">
7933.               <item>
7934.                 <term><see langword="null"/></term>
7935.                 <description>Returns an empty <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResponse.Content"/> list.</description>
7936.               </item>
7937.               <item>
7938.                 <term><see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent"/></term>
7939.                 <description>Converted to a single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Content"/> object using <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.AIContentExtensions.ToContent(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent)"/>.</description>
7940.               </item>
7941.               <item>
7942.                 <term><see cref="T:System.String"/></term>
7943.                 <description>Converted to a single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Content"/> object with <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Content.Text"/> set to the string value and <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Content.Type"/> set to "text".</description>
7944.               </item>
7945.               <item>
7946.                 <term><see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Content"/></term>
7947.                 <description>Returned as a single-item <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Content"/> list.</description>
7948.               </item>
7949.               <item>
7950.                 <term><see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> of <see cref="T:System.String"/></term>

                   what server is being used to process the request, and to interact with the client issuing the request to that server.
                 </description>
               </item>
               <item>
                 <description>
                   <see cref="T:System.IProgress`1"/> parameters accepting <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.ProgressNotificationValue"/> values
                   are not included in the JSON schema and are bound to an <see cref="T:System.IProgress`1"/> instance manufactured
                   to forward progress notifications from the tool to the client. If the client included a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressToken"/> in their request, 
                   progress reports issued to this instance will propagate to the client as <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ProgressNotification"/> notifications with
                   that token. If the client did not include a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressToken"/>, the instance will ignore any progress reports issued to it.
                 </description>
               </item>
               <item>
                 <description>
                   When the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> is constructed, it may be passed an <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> via 
                   <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolCreateOptions.Services"/>. Any parameter that can be satisfied by that <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/>
                   according to <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceProviderIsService"/> will not be included in the generated JSON schema and will be resolved 
                   from the <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> provided to when the tool is invoked rather than from the argument collection.
                 </description>
               </item>
               <item>
                 <description>
                   Any parameter attributed with <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.FromKeyedServicesAttribute"/> will similarly be resolved from the 
                   <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> provided when the tool is invoked rather than from the argument
                   collection, and will not be included in the generated JSON schema.
                 </description>
               </item>
             </list>
             </para>
             <para>
             All other parameters are deserialized from the <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/>s in the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams.Arguments"/> dictionary, 
             using the <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions"/> supplied in <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolCreateOptions.SerializerOptions"/>, or if none was provided, 
             using <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.McpJsonUtilities.DefaultOptions"/>.
             </para>
             <para>
             In general, the data supplied via the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams.Arguments"/>'s dictionary is passed along from the caller and
             should thus be considered unvalidated and untrusted. To provide validated and trusted data to the invocation of the tool, consider having 
             the tool be an instance method, referring to data stored in the instance, or using an instance or parameters resolved from the <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/>
             to provide data to the method.
             </para>
             <para>
             Return values from a method are used to create the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResponse"/> that is sent back to the client:
             </para>
             <list type="table">
               <item>
                 <term><see langword="null"/></term>
                 <description>Returns an empty <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResponse.Content"/> list.</description>
               </item>
               <item>
                 <term><see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent"/></term>
                 <description>Converted to a single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Content"/> object using <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.AIContentExtensions.ToContent(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent)"/>.</description>
               </item>
               <item>
                 <term><see cref="T:System.String"/></term>
                 <description>Converted to a single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Content"/> object with <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Content.Text"/> set to the string value and <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Content.Type"/> set to "text".</description>
               </item>
               <item>
                 <term><see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Content"/></term>
                 <description>Returned as a single-item <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Content"/> list.</description>
               </item>
               <item>
                 <term><see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> of <see cref="T:System.String"/></term>

import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
import re

xml_file = "/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.core/0.2.0-preview.3/lib/net9.0/ModelContextProtocol.Core.xml"
tree = ET.parse(xml_file)
root = tree.getroot()

# Find key attribute members
attributes_to_find = [
    ("McpServerToolAttribute", "Tool"),
    ("McpServerToolTypeAttribute", "Tool Type"),
    ("McpServerResourceAttribute", "Resource"),
    ("McpServerResourceTypeAttribute", "Resource Type"),
    ("McpServerPromptAttribute", "Prompt"),
    ("McpServerPromptTypeAttribute", "Prompt Type"),
]

for attr_name, display_name in attributes_to_find:
    print(f"\n{'=' * 80}")
    print(f"{display_name} Attribute: {attr_name}")
    print('=' * 80)
    
    # Find the type member
    for member in root.findall('.//member'):
        name = member.get('name', '')
        if f'T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.{attr_name}' == name:
            # Get summary and remarks
            summary = member.findtext('summary', '').strip()
            if summary:
                print(f"\nSummary:\n{summary[:500]}")
            
            # Find all properties/methods for this type
            continue
    
    # Find properties and methods
    for member in root.findall('.//member'):
        name = member.get('name', '')
        if f'ModelContextProtocol.Server.{attr_name}' in name and not name.endswith(f'T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.{attr_name}'):
            if '.P:' in name or '.M:' in name:  # Properties or Methods
                member_type = 'P' if '.P:' in name else 'M'
                member_name = name.split('.')[-1]
                
                if member_type == 'P':
                    summary = member.findtext('summary', '').strip()
                    print(f"\n  Property: {member_name}")
                    if summary:
                        print(f"    {summary[:80]}")
                elif member_type == 'M' and not member_name.startswith('.'):
                    # Skip constructors for now, we'll show them separately
                    pass

7981.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.Title">
7982.             <summary>
7983.             Gets or sets a human-readable title for the tool that can be displayed to users.
7984.             </summary>
7985.             <remarks>
7986.             <para>
7987.             The title provides a more descriptive, user-friendly name for the tool than the tool's
7988.             programmatic name. It is intended for display purposes and to help users understand
7989.             the tool's purpose at a glance.
7990.             </para>
7991.             <para>
7992.             Unlike the tool name (which follows programmatic naming conventions), the title can
7993.             include spaces, special characters, and be phrased in a more natural language style.
7994.             </para>
7995.             </remarks>
7996.         </member>
7997.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.Destructive">
7998.             <summary>
7999.             Gets or sets whether the tool may perform destructive updates to its environment.
8000.             </summary>
8001.             <remarks>
8002.             <para>
8003.             If <see langword="true"/>, the tool may perform destructive updates to its environment.
8004.             If <see langword="false"/>, the tool performs only additive updates.
8005.             This property is most relevant when the tool modifies its environment (ReadOnly = false).
8006.             </para>
8007.             <para>
8008.             The default is <see langword="true"/>.
8009.             </para>
8010.             </remarks>
8011.         </member>
8012.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.Idempotent">
8013.             <summary>
8014.             Gets or sets whether calling the tool repeatedly with the same arguments 
8015.             will have no additional effect on its environment.
8016.             </summary>
8017.             <remarks>
8018.             <para>
8019.             This property is most relevant when the tool modifies its environment (ReadOnly = false).
8020.             </para>
8021.             <para>
8022.             The default is <see langword="false"/>.
8023.             </para>
8024.             </remarks>
8025.         </member>
8026.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.OpenWorld">
8027.             <summary>
8028.             Gets or sets whether this tool may interact with an "open world" of external entities.
8029.             </summary>
8030.             <remarks>
8031.             <para>
8032.             If <see langword="true"/>, the tool may interact with an unpredictable or dynamic set of entities (like web search).
8033.             If <see langword="false"/>, the tool's domain of interaction is closed and well-defined (like memory access).
8034.             </para>
8035.             <para>
8036.             The default is <see langword="true"/>.
8037.             </para>
8038.             </remarks>
8039.         </member>
8040.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.ReadOnly">
8041.             <summary>
8042.             Gets or sets whether this tool does not modify its environment.
8043.             </summary>
8044.             <remarks>
8045.             <para>
8046.             If <see langword="true"/>, the tool only performs read operations without changing state.
8047.             If <see langword="false"/>, the tool may make modifications to its environment.
8048.             </para>
8049.             <para>
8050.             Read-only tools do not have side effects beyond computational resource usage.

data.reasoningText:
The `McpServerResource` attribute has a `Description` property. But the error says `Description` type is not found. This suggests the `McpServerResourceAttribute` itself doesn't have a `Description` property - maybe it uses a separate `[Description]` attribute instead, or the property is named differently. Let me check.

The error is on line 36 which is `Description = "Returns the current application configuration."`. This looks like a named parameter to the `McpServerResourceAttribute`. Maybe the attribute doesn't have that property. Let me check.

Actually wait, looking at it more carefully - the `Description` here is a named parameter of `McpServerResource(...)`. 

The compiler error about `Description` not being found is strange for a named parameter. I notice that `[Description("...")]` works fine on the tool methods because we have `using System.ComponentModel`, but inside the `[McpServerResource(...)]` constructor on line 36, if the attribute doesn't define a `Description` property, I'd expect a different error. Let me look at the exact error message again to understand what's happening.

Actually, I think the issue is that `McpServerResourceAttribute` might not have a `Description` property at all. Rather than trying to use it as a named parameter there, I should remove it from the attribute and apply `[Description]` separately instead, the way we're doing it for the tool methods.

================================================================================
ModelContextProtocol 0.2.0-preview.3 - PUBLIC API SURFACE
================================================================================

PACKAGE LOCATIONS:
- ModelContextProtocol: ~/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/0.2.0-preview.3/
- ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore: ~/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.aspnetcore/0.2.0-preview.3/
- ModelContextProtocol.Core: ~/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.core/0.2.0-preview.3/

================================================================================
1. DEPENDENCY INJECTION REGISTRATION (AddMcpServer)
================================================================================

NAMESPACE: Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection

Method:
  IServiceCollection.AddMcpServer(Action<McpServerOptions> configureOptions = null)
  → Returns: IMcpServerBuilder

Properties:
  IMcpServerBuilder.Services (IServiceCollection)

Builder Methods:
  - .WithTools<TToolType>(JsonSerializerOptions serializerOptions = null)
  - .WithTools(IEnumerable<McpServerTool> tools)
  - .WithTools(IEnumerable<Type> toolTypes, JsonSerializerOptions serializerOptions = null)
  - .WithToolsFromAssembly(Assembly toolAssembly = null, JsonSerializerOptions serializerOptions = null)
  
  - .WithResources<TResourceType>()
  - .WithResources(IEnumerable<McpServerResource> resourceTemplates)
  - .WithResources(IEnumerable<Type> resourceTemplateTypes)
  - .WithResourcesFromAssembly(Assembly resourceAssembly = null)
  
  - .WithPrompts<TPromptType>(JsonSerializerOptions serializerOptions = null)
  - .WithPrompts(IEnumerable<McpServerPrompt> prompts)
  - .WithPrompts(IEnumerable<Type> promptTypes, JsonSerializerOptions serializerOptions = null)
  - .WithPromptsFromAssembly(Assembly promptAssembly = null, JsonSerializerOptions serializerOptions = null)
  
  - .WithStdioServerTransport()
  - .WithStreamServerTransport(Stream inputStream, Stream outputStream)
  
  [Handler Configuration Methods - all return IMcpServerBuilder]
  - .WithListToolsHandler(handler)
  - .WithCallToolHandler(handler)
  - .WithListPromptsHandler(handler)
  - .WithGetPromptHandler(handler)
  - .WithListResourceTemplatesHandler(handler)
  - .WithListResourcesHandler(handler)
  - .WithReadResourceHandler(handler)
  - .WithCompleteHandler(handler)
  - .WithSubscribeToResourcesHandler(handler)
  - .WithUnsubscribeFromResourcesHandler(handler)
  - .WithSetLoggingLevelHandler(handler)

================================================================================
2. HTTP ENDPOINT MAPPING (MapMcp) - AspNetCore
================================================================================

NAMESPACE: Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder

Method:
  IEndpointRouteBuilder.MapMcp(string pattern = "/mcp")
  → Returns: IEndpointConventionBuilder

HTTP Configuration:
  IEndpointRouteBuilder.Services.AddMcpServer()
    .WithHttpTransport(Action<HttpServerTransportOptions> configureOptions = null)

Configuration Class:
  HttpServerTransportOptions
  - Property: ConfigureSessionOptions (async callback for per-session McpServerOptions)
  - Property: RunSessionHandler (async callback to run new MCP sessions)
  - Property: Stateless (bool, default false) - run stateless mode without per-process session storage
  - Property: IdleTimeout (TimeSpan, default 2 hours)
  - Property: MaxIdleSessionCount (int, default 100,000)
  - Property: TimeProvider (for testing)

Usage Example:
  var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);
  builder.Services
    .AddMcpServer()
    .WithHttpTransport()
    .WithToolsFromAssembly();
  
  var app = builder.Build();
  app.MapMcp();

================================================================================
3. TOOL DEFINITION ATTRIBUTES
================================================================================

NAMESPACE: ModelContextProtocol.Server

[McpServerTool]
  - Applied to: Methods
  - Container Type: Class marked with [McpServerToolType]
  - Properties:
    * Name: string - Gets the tool name (if null, method name is used)
    * Title: string - Human-readable title for display
    * Destructive: bool (default true) - May perform destructive updates
    * Idempotent: bool (default false) - Repeated calls have no additional effect
    * OpenWorld: bool (default true) - May interact with open world of external entities
    * ReadOnly: bool (default false) - Does not modify environment

[McpServerToolType]
  - Applied to: Class/static class containing [McpServerTool] methods
  - Used to mark a type for automatic tool discovery

McpServerToolAttribute Constructor:
  public McpServerToolAttribute()  // No parameters required

Parameter Resolution (for tool methods):
  - CancellationToken → Bound automatically to cancellation token
  - IServiceProvider → Bound from RequestContext
  - IMcpServer → Bound to the current server instance
  - IProgress<ProgressNotificationValue> → Progress reporting
  - Services from IServiceProvider (if available)
  - Parameters with [FromKeyedServices] attribute
  - Other parameters → Deserialized from Arguments dictionary

Return Value Conversion:
  - null → Empty content list
  - string → TextContent
  - Content → Single item list
  - IEnumerable<string> → Multiple TextContent items
  - IEnumerable<Content> → Returned as-is
  - AIContent → Converted via ToContent()

================================================================================
4. RESOURCE DEFINITION ATTRIBUTES
================================================================================

NAMESPACE: ModelContextProtocol.Server

[McpServerResource]
  - Applied to: Methods or Properties
  - Container Type: Class marked with [McpServerResourceType]
  - Properties:
    * UriTemplate: string - RFC 6570 URI template (if null, derived from name/params)
    * Name: string - Resource name (if null, method/property name is used)
    * MimeType: string - MIME type of resource

[McpServerResourceType]
  - Applied to: Class/static class containing [McpServerResource] methods/properties
  - Used to mark a type for automatic resource discovery

McpServerResourceAttribute Constructor:
  public McpServerResourceAttribute()  // No parameters required

Resource Classification:
  - With UriTemplate containing parameters → "Resource Template" (listed via list_resourceTemplates)
  - Without parameters → "Direct Resource" (listed via list_resources)

Parameter Resolution (for resource methods):
  - CancellationToken → Bound automatically
  - IServiceProvider → Bound from RequestContext
  - IMcpServer → Bound to the current server instance
  - Services from IServiceProvider (if available)
  - Parameters with [FromKeyedServices] attribute
  - Other parameters → Bound from URI data

Return Value Conversion:
  - ResourceContents → Wrapped in list
  - TextContent → Single TextResourceContents
  - DataContent → Single BlobResourceContents
  - string → TextResourceContents
  - IEnumerable<ResourceContents> → Returned as-is
  - IEnumerable<AIContent> → Converted to appropriate ResourceContents
  - IEnumerable<string> → TextResourceContents for each

================================================================================
5. PROMPT DEFINITION ATTRIBUTES
================================================================================

NAMESPACE: ModelContextProtocol.Server

[McpServerPrompt]
  - Applied to: Methods
  - Container Type: Class marked with [McpServerPromptType]
  - Properties:
    * Name: string - Gets the prompt name (if null, method name is used)

[McpServerPromptType]
  - Applied to: Class/static class containing [McpServerPrompt] methods
  - Used to mark a type for automatic prompt discovery

McpServerPromptAttribute Constructor:
  public McpServerPromptAttribute()  // No parameters required

Parameter Resolution (for prompt methods):
  - CancellationToken → Bound automatically
  - IServiceProvider → Bound from RequestContext
  - IMcpServer → Bound to the current server instance
  - Services from IServiceProvider (if available)
  - Parameters with [FromKeyedServices] attribute
  - Other parameters → Deserialized from Arguments dictionary

Return Value Conversion:
  - string → Single PromptMessage
  - PromptMessage → Single item list
  - IEnumerable<PromptMessage> → Returned as-is
  - ChatMessage → Converted to PromptMessage list
  - IEnumerable<ChatMessage> → Converted to PromptMessage list

================================================================================
6. KEY RESOURCE/TEMPLATE CLASSES
================================================================================

NAMESPACE: ModelContextProtocol.Server

McpServerResource
  - Represents an invocable resource
  - Used for both resource templates and direct resources
  - Methods: McpServerResource.Create(...) for programmatic creation

McpServerResourceCreateOptions
  - Services: IServiceProvider - Services for dependency injection
  - Name: string - Resource name override
  - Description: string - Resource description
  - MimeType: string - MIME type

McpServerTool
  - Represents an invocable tool
  - Methods: McpServerTool.Create(...) for programmatic creation

McpServerToolCreateOptions
  - Services: IServiceProvider - Services for dependency injection
  - Name: string - Tool name override
  - Description: string - Tool description
  - Title: string - Human-readable title

McpServerPrompt
  - Represents an invocable prompt
  - Methods: McpServerPrompt.Create(...) for programmatic creation

McpServerPromptCreateOptions
  - Services: IServiceProvider - Services for dependency injection
  - Name: string - Prompt name override
  - Description: string - Prompt description

================================================================================
7. USAGE EXAMPLES
================================================================================

BASIC SETUP WITH ATTRIBUTES:

[McpServerToolType]
public static class MyTools
{
    [McpServerTool, Description("Echoes the message back")]
    public static string Echo(string message) => $"Echo: {message}";
}

[McpServerResourceType]
public static class MyResources
{
    [McpServerResource(UriTemplate = "file://{path}")]
    public static string ReadFile(string path) 
        => System.IO.File.ReadAllText(path);
}

REGISTRATION:

var builder = Host.CreateApplicationBuilder();
builder.Services
    .AddMcpServer()
    .WithStdioServerTransport()
    .WithToolsFromAssembly()
    .WithResourcesFromAssembly();

WEB APPLICATION:

var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder();
builder.Services
    .AddMcpServer()
    .WithHttpTransport(options =>
    {
        options.IdleTimeout = TimeSpan.FromHours(1);
    })
    .WithToolsFromAssembly()
    .WithResourcesFromAssembly();

var app = builder.Build();
app.MapMcp("/mcp");  // Maps to /mcp endpoint
app.Run();

================================================================================
SUMMARY
================================================================================

Key Points:
1. AddMcpServer() returns an IMcpServerBuilder for fluent configuration
2. Tools, Resources, and Prompts can be defined using attributes or programmatically
3. HTTP servers use MapMcp() to set up endpoints for the Streamable HTTP transport
4. Resource templates use URI templates (RFC 6570) to define parameterized resources
5. Parameter binding is automatic for common types (CancellationToken, IMcpServer, etc.)
6. AspNetCore transport supports stateless mode for distributed scenarios
7. All attribute names follow the pattern: [McpServer<Type>Attribute] and [McpServer<Type>Type]
