  - .WithListToolsHandler(handler)
  - .WithCallToolHandler(handler)
  - .WithListPromptsHandler(handler)
  - .WithGetPromptHandler(handler)
  - .WithListResourceTemplatesHandler(handler)
  - .WithListResourcesHandler(handler)
  - .WithReadResourceHandler(handler)
  - .WithCompleteHandler(handler)
  - .WithSubscribeToResourcesHandler(handler)
  - .WithUnsubscribeFromResourcesHandler(handler)
  - .WithSetLoggingLevelHandler(handler)

================================================================================
2. HTTP ENDPOINT MAPPING (MapMcp) - AspNetCore
================================================================================

NAMESPACE: Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder

Method:
  IEndpointRouteBuilder.MapMcp(string pattern = "/mcp")
  → Returns: IEndpointConventionBuilder

HTTP Configuration:
  IEndpointRouteBuilder.Services.AddMcpServer()
    .WithHttpTransport(Action<HttpServerTransportOptions> configureOptions = null)

Configuration Class:
  HttpServerTransportOptions
  - Property: ConfigureSessionOptions (async callback for per-session McpServerOptions)
  - Property: RunSessionHandler (async callback to run new MCP sessions)
  - Property: Stateless (bool, default false) - run stateless mode without per-process session storage
  - Property: IdleTimeout (TimeSpan, default 2 hours)
  - Property: MaxIdleSessionCount (int, default 100,000)
  - Property: TimeProvider (for testing)

Usage Example:
  var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);
  builder.Services
    .AddMcpServer()
    .WithHttpTransport()
    .WithToolsFromAssembly();
  
  var app = builder.Build();
  app.MapMcp();

================================================================================
3. TOOL DEFINITION ATTRIBUTES
================================================================================

NAMESPACE: ModelContextProtocol.Server

[McpServerTool]
  - Applied to: Methods
  - Container Type: Class marked with [McpServerToolType]
  - Properties:
    * Name: string - Gets the tool name (if null, method name is used)
    * Title: string - Human-readable title for display
    * Destructive: bool (default true) - May perform destructive updates
    * Idempotent: bool (default false) - Repeated calls have no additional effect
    * OpenWorld: bool (default true) - May interact with open world of external entities
    * ReadOnly: bool (default false) - Does not modify environment

[McpServerToolType]
  - Applied to: Class/static class containing [McpServerTool] methods
  - Used to mark a type for automatic tool discovery

McpServerToolAttribute Constructor:
  public McpServerToolAttribute()  // No parameters required

Parameter Resolution (for tool methods):
  - CancellationToken → Bound automatically to cancellation token
  - IServiceProvider → Bound from RequestContext
  - IMcpServer → Bound to the current server instance
  - IProgress<ProgressNotificationValue> → Progress reporting
  - Services from IServiceProvider (if available)
  - Parameters with [FromKeyedServices] attribute
  - Other parameters → Deserialized from Arguments dictionary

Return Value Conversion:
  - null → Empty content list
  - string → TextContent
  - Content → Single item list
  - IEnumerable<string> → Multiple TextContent items
  - IEnumerable<Content> → Returned as-is
  - AIContent → Converted via ToContent()

================================================================================
4. RESOURCE DEFINITION ATTRIBUTES
================================================================================

NAMESPACE: ModelContextProtocol.Server

[McpServerResource]
  - Applied to: Methods or Properties
  - Container Type: Class marked with [McpServerResourceType]
  - Properties:
    * UriTemplate: string - RFC 6570 URI template (if null, derived from name/params)
    * Name: string - Resource name (if null, method/property name is used)
    * MimeType: string - MIME type of resource

[McpServerResourceType]
  - Applied to: Class/static class containing [McpServerResource] methods/properties
  - Used to mark a type for automatic resource discovery

McpServerResourceAttribute Constructor:
  public McpServerResourceAttribute()  // No parameters required

Resource Classification:
  - With UriTemplate containing parameters → "Resource Template" (listed via list_resourceTemplates)
  - Without parameters → "Direct Resource" (listed via list_resources)

Parameter Resolution (for resource methods):
  - CancellationToken → Bound automatically
  - IServiceProvider → Bound from RequestContext
  - IMcpServer → Bound to the current server instance
  - Services from IServiceProvider (if available)
  - Parameters with [FromKeyedServices] attribute
  - Other parameters → Bound from URI data

Return Value Conversion:
  - ResourceContents → Wrapped in list
  - TextContent → Single TextResourceContents
  - DataContent → Single BlobResourceContents
  - string → TextResourceContents
  - IEnumerable<ResourceContents> → Returned as-is
  - IEnumerable<AIContent> → Converted to appropriate ResourceContents
  - IEnumerable<string> → TextResourceContents for each

================================================================================
5. PROMPT DEFINITION ATTRIBUTES
================================================================================

NAMESPACE: ModelContextProtocol.Server

[McpServerPrompt]
  - Applied to: Methods
  - Container Type: Class marked with [McpServerPromptType]
  - Properties:
    * Name: string - Gets the prompt name (if null, method name is used)

[McpServerPromptType]
  - Applied to: Class/static class containing [McpServerPrompt] methods
  - Used to mark a type for automatic prompt discovery

McpServerPromptAttribute Constructor:
  public McpServerPromptAttribute()  // No parameters required

Parameter Resolution (for prompt methods):
  - CancellationToken → Bound automatically
  - IServiceProvider → Bound from RequestContext
  - IMcpServer → Bound to the current server instance
  - Services from IServiceProvider (if available)
  - Parameters with [FromKeyedServices] attribute
  - Other parameters → Deserialized from Arguments dictionary

Return Value Conversion:
  - string → Single PromptMessage
  - PromptMessage → Single item list
  - IEnumerable<PromptMessage> → Returned as-is
  - ChatMessage → Converted to PromptMessage list
  - IEnumerable<ChatMessage> → Converted to PromptMessage list

================================================================================
6. KEY RESOURCE/TEMPLATE CLASSES
================================================================================

NAMESPACE: ModelContextProtocol.Server

McpServerResource
  - Represents an invocable resource
  - Used for both resource templates and direct resources
  - Methods: McpServerResource.Create(...) for programmatic creation

McpServerResourceCreateOptions
  - Services: IServiceProvider - Services for dependency injection
  - Name: string - Resource name override
  - Description: string - Resource description
  - MimeType: string - MIME type

McpServerTool
  - Represents an invocable tool
  - Methods: McpServerTool.Create(...) for programmatic creation

McpServerToolCreateOptions
  - Services: IServiceProvider - Services for dependency injection
  - Name: string - Tool name override
  - Description: string - Tool description
  - Title: string - Human-readable title

McpServerPrompt
  - Represents an invocable prompt
  - Methods: McpServerPrompt.Create(...) for programmatic creation

McpServerPromptCreateOptions
  - Services: IServiceProvider - Services for dependency injection
  - Name: string - Prompt name override
  - Description: string - Prompt description

================================================================================
7. USAGE EXAMPLES
================================================================================

BASIC SETUP WITH ATTRIBUTES:

[McpServerToolType]
public static class MyTools
{
    [McpServerTool, Description("Echoes the message back")]
    public static string Echo(string message) => $"Echo: {message}";
}

[McpServerResourceType]
public static class MyResources
{
    [McpServerResource(UriTemplate = "file://{path}")]
    public static string ReadFile(string path) 
        => System.IO.File.ReadAllText(path);
}

REGISTRATION:

var builder = Host.CreateApplicationBuilder();
builder.Services
    .AddMcpServer()
    .WithStdioServerTransport()
    .WithToolsFromAssembly()
    .WithResourcesFromAssembly();

WEB APPLICATION:

var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder();
builder.Services
    .AddMcpServer()
    .WithHttpTransport(options =>
    {
        options.IdleTimeout = TimeSpan.FromHours(1);
    })
    .WithToolsFromAssembly()
    .WithResourcesFromAssembly();

var app = builder.Build();
app.MapMcp("/mcp");  // Maps to /mcp endpoint
app.Run();

================================================================================
SUMMARY
================================================================================

Key Points:
1. AddMcpServer() returns an IMcpServerBuilder for fluent configuration
2. Tools, Resources, and Prompts can be defined using attributes or programmatically
3. HTTP servers use MapMcp() to set up endpoints for the Streamable HTTP transport
4. Resource templates use URI templates (RFC 6570) to define parameterized resources
5. Parameter binding is automatic for common types (CancellationToken, IMcpServer, etc.)
6. AspNetCore transport supports stateless mode for distributed scenarios
7. All attribute names follow the pattern: [McpServer<Type>Attribute] and [McpServer<Type>Type]

kind.prompt:
In the NuGet package cache, find the ModelContextProtocol and ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore assemblies (version 0.2.0-preview*) and list their public API surface. I need to know:

1. How to register MCP server in DI (AddMcpServer and its builder methods like WithTools, WithResources, etc.)
2. How to map the HTTP endpoint (MapMcp)
3. How to define tools using attributes ([McpServerTool], [McpServerToolType], etc.)
4. How to define resources using attributes ([McpServerResource], [McpServerResourceType], [McpServerResourceTemplate], etc.)
5. What resource-related interfaces/classes exist (McpServerResource, ResourceTemplate, etc.)

Search in ~/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/ and ~/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.aspnetcore/ for source or decompiled XML docs. Also look for any README or docs files in the package.

Focus on finding the exact API signatures and attribute names.
